trigger_preview.description: Set to true when you have enough context to generate a meaningful table structure. Set to false if you need more information from the user.

follow_up_question.description: If trigger_preview is false, ask a focused question to gather more context. If trigger_preview is true, this should contain your proposal: 'Here is what I understand so far: {description of the table}. Shall I generate a preview for you to review? We can refine the table if it is not what you are expecting.' Use markdown formatting (bold, lists, bullets) for clarity.

context_web_research.description: List of specific research questions that help define the COLUMNS and provide context for table generation (NOT about the rows themselves). These should be research questions that will inform column descriptions and configuration. Examples: 'What metrics define GenAI job postings?', 'What are standard citation metrics for research papers?', 'What validation criteria exist for AI company claims?'. These will be researched by the preview generator and embedded in column descriptions. Leave empty if no research needed.

processing_steps.description: List of 3-5 word action phrases describing the steps you'll take to generate the table. These should be specific to the problem at hand. Examples: 'Researching Eliyahu.AI', 'Looking for Job Applications', 'Considering Applicability', 'Extracting Paper Citations', 'Validating Company Information'.

table_name.description: Short, descriptive table name in proper title case (e.g., 'Research Applications for Internal Programs', 'New Clients with GenAI Job Postings'). Use clear, human-readable titles that describe the table's purpose. This will be converted to snake_case for file naming.
